title: Antiretroviral therapy coverage (% of people living with HIV)
definition: Antiretroviral therapy coverage indicates the percentage of all people living with HIV who are receiving antiretroviral therapy.
unit: percent of people living with HIV

source.license: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International unless specifically labeled otherwise by World Bank metadata
source.sourceOrganization: UNAIDS estimates, Joint United Nations Programme on HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS), uri: https://aidsinfo.unaids.org/, publisher: UNAIDS, date accessed: 2025-08-27, date published: 2025-07

editorial.patternNote: The darkest countries include Ireland, Rwanda, Zimbabwe, while the lightest include Afghanistan, Pakistan, Sudan; that contrast is the fastest way to read the antiretroviral therapy coverage pattern.
editorial.whyItMatters: Antiretroviral therapy coverage offers a health-system access clue that is different from incidence or mortality.

bestProbeCountries.reason: High anchor: this country sits near the darkest end of the map.
Low anchor: this country clarifies the lightest end of the scale.
Middle check: this country helps separate a true pattern from one-off outliers.

commonConfusions.reason: Female primary pupils overlaps with part of the visible pattern, but the full country spread does not match.
Female-to-male labor force ratio overlaps with part of the visible pattern, but the full country spread does not match.
Female labor force participation overlaps with part of the visible pattern, but the full country spread does not match.

editorial.difficultyReason: Expert difficulty: the map can be mistaken for adjacent indicators unless players probe a high, low, and middle country.
editorial.dataCaveat: Coverage is lower than broad modeled health indicators and survey/reporting cadence varies.
regionalSignals: Health maps often separate income, public-health capacity, and conflict exposure.
Check both high-income and low-income anchors.

reviewNotes: Batch 4 QA: useful treatment-access map but narrower than broad health-system indicators.
Expert-only until HIV-related distractors and reveal wording are reviewed together.
